title: Coursetia ferruginea
intro: Coursetia ferruginea, also known as the ironwood tree, is a small, deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its use in treating various health issues such as fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Coursetia ferruginea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Coursetia ferruginea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Coursetia, which is a group of plants that are primarily found in the Americas. The family Fabaceae is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with over 19,000 known species. Coursetia ferruginea is native to the Americas and is often found in dry and rocky areas. It is a low-growing shrub with small, yellow flowers.
